old.claim_text:
(SS) female camp guards removing prisoners' bodies from lorries and carrying them to a mass grave, inside the German Bergen-Belsen concentration camp, 1945]]

Nazi Germany, under the dictatorship of Adolf Hitler, was responsible for the Holocaust (which killed approximately 6million Jews) as well as for killing 2.7 million ethnic Poles and 4million others who were deemed "unworthy of life" (including the disabled and mentally ill, Soviet prisoners of war, Romani, homosexuals, Freemasons, and Jehovah's Witnesses) as part of a programme of deliberate extermination, in effect becoming a "genocidal state".
old.subject: (SS) female camp guards
old.source_section: Genocide, concentration camps, and slave labour

old.claim_text:
A denazification programme in Germany led to the prosecution of Nazi war criminals in the Nuremberg trials and the removal of ex-Nazis from power, although this policy moved towards amnesty and re-integration of ex-Nazis into West German society..

Germany lost a quarter of its pre-war (1937) territory.

On 16 December 1944, Germany made a last attempt on the Western Front by using most of its remaining reserves to launch a massive counter-offensive in the Ardennes and along with the French-German border to split the Western Allies, encircle large portions of Western Allied troops and capture their primary supply port at Antwerp to prompt a political settlement.
old.subject: Germany

held in February 1945, with Winston Churchill, Franklin D. Roosevelt and Joseph Stalin]]

On 16 December 1944, Germany made a last attempt on the Western Front by using most of its remaining reserves to launch a massive counter-offensive in the Ardennes and along the French-German border to split the Western Allies, encircle large portions of Western Allied troops and capture their primary supply port at Antwerp to prompt a political settlement.
new.subject: Germany

old.claim_text: Key setbacks in 1943—including a series of German defeats on the Eastern Front, the Allied invasions of Sicily and the Italian mainland, and Allied offensives in the Pacific—cost the Axis powers their initiative and forced it into strategic retreat on all fronts.
old.subject: Key setbacks

new.claim_text: Key setbacks in 1943—including a series of German defeats on the Eastern Front, the Allied invasions of Sicily and the Italian mainland, and Allied offensives in the Pacific—cost the Axis powers their initiative and forced them into strategic retreat on all fronts.

old.claim_text: From late 1939 to early 1941, in a series of campaigns and treaties, Germany conquered or controlled much of continental Europe, and formed the Axis alliance with Italy and Japan (along with other countries later on).
old.subject: Germany

old.claim_text: The United States produced about two-thirds of all the munitions used by the Allies in WWII, including warships, transports, warplanes, artillery, tanks, trucks, and ammunition.

old.claim_text: World War II or the Second World War, often abbreviated as WWII or WW2, was a global war that lasted from 1939 to 1945.
old.subject: World War II

new.claim_text: World War II or the Second World War, often abbreviated as WWII or WW2, was a world war that lasted from 1939 to 1945.

old.claim_text: By January, the offensive had been repulsed with no strategic objectives fulfilled.
old.subject: the offensive

new.claim_text: By 16 January 1945, the offensive had been repulsed with no strategic objectives fulfilled.

old.claim_text: World War II was by far the deadliest conflict in human history; it resulted in 70 to 85 million fatalities, a majority being civilians.
old.subject: it

new.claim_text: World War II was by far the deadliest conflict in human history; it resulted in 70 to 85 million fatalities, mostly among civilians.
new.subject: it

old.claim_text: Aircraft played a major role in the conflict, enabling the strategic bombing of population centres and the only two uses of nuclear weapons in war.
old.subject: Aircraft

new.claim_text: Aircraft played a major role in the conflict, enabling the strategic bombing of population centres and deploying the only two nuclear weapons ever used in war.
new.subject: Aircraft

old.claim_text: On 12 April, President Roosevelt died and was succeeded by Harry S. Truman.

new.claim_text: On 12 April, President Roosevelt died and was succeeded by his vice president, Harry S. Truman.

new.claim_text: On 30 April, Hitler committed suicide in his headquarters, and he was succeeded by Grand Admiral Karl Dönitz and Joseph Goebbels.

old.claim_text: Faced with an imminent invasion of the Japanese archipelago, the possibility of additional atomic bombings, and the Soviet entry into the war against Japan and its invasion of Manchuria, Japan announced its intention to surrender on 15 August, then signed the surrender document on 2 September 1945, cementing total victory in Asia for the Allies.
old.subject: Japan

new.claim_text: Faced with an imminent invasion of the Japanese archipelago, the possibility of additional atomic bombings, and the Soviet Union's declared entry into the war against Japan on the eve of invading Manchuria, Japan announced on 10 August its intention to surrender, signing a surrender document on 2 September 1945.
new.subject: Japan

qualifiers.spatial: the Soviet Union's, Japan, Manchuria, Japan

added.claim_text: World War II was a total war that directly involved more than 100 million personnel from more than 30 countries.
added.subject: World War II

added.claim_text: Tens of millions died due to genocides (including the Holocaust), starvation, massacres, and disease.
added.subject: Tens

added.claim_text: The causes of World War II are debated, but contributing factors included the Second Italo-Ethiopian War, Spanish Civil War, Second Sino-Japanese War, Soviet–Japanese border conflicts, rise of fascism in Europe and rising European tensions since World War I. World War II is generally considered to have begun on 1 September 1939, when Nazi Germany, under Adolf Hitler, invaded Poland.
added.subject: The causes

added.claim_text: In December 1941, Japan attacked American and British territories with near-simultaneous offensives against Southeast Asia and the Central Pacific, including an attack on the US fleet at Pearl Harbor which resulted in the United States declaring war against Japan.
added.subject: Japan

qualifiers.spatial: Japan, Southeast Asia, the Central Pacific, US, Pearl Harbor, the United States, Japan

added.claim_text: The European Axis powers declared war on the United States in solidarity.
added.subject: The European Axis powers

added.claim_text: Other dates for the beginning of the war in the Pacific include the start of the Second Sino-Japanese War on 7 July 1937, [REF:
added.subject: Other dates

added.claim_text: During the Phoney War period, the alliance provided no direct military support to Poland, outside of a cautious French probe into the Saarland.., observes that, while it is true that Poland was far away, making it difficult for the French and British to provide support, "[f]ew Western historians of World War II ... know that the British had committed to bomb Germany if it attacked Poland, but did not do so except for one raid on the base of Wilhelmshaven.
added.subject: the alliance

qualifiers.spatial: Poland, Saarland, Poland, Germany, Poland, Wilhelmshaven
qualifiers.conditional: if it attacked Poland, but did not do so except for one raid on the base of Wilhelmshaven.

added.claim_text: Major changes in leadership occurred on both sides during this period.
added.subject: Major changes

in 1945, after the deliberate destruction of the city by the occupying German forces]]

The Allies established occupation administrations in Austria and Germany, both initially divided between western and eastern occupation zones controlled by the Western Allies and the Soviet Union, respectively.
added.subject: both

added.claim_text: In Germany, the western and eastern occupation zones controlled by the Western Allies and the Soviet Union officially ended in 1949, with the respective zones becoming separate countries, West Germany and East Germany.
added.subject: the western and eastern occupation zones

qualifiers.spatial: Germany, the Soviet Union, West Germany, East Germany

added.claim_text: However, in Austria occupation continued until 1955, when a joint settlement between the Western Allies and the Soviet Union permitted the reunification of Austria as a neutral democratic state, officially non-aligned with any political bloc (although in practice having better relations with the Western Allies).
added.subject: Austria occupation
